arguments.task: Visit official Asana pages and extract evidence for: API auth methods, getting started/auth flows, pricing or access requirements for API use, rate limits, API reference surface (REST/GraphQL), and whether Asana has an official MCP server or documented MCP. Prefer developers.asana.com, developers.asana.com/docs, app.asana.com/api/1.0 docs, Asana pricing/help/changelog, official GitHub, and official MCP registry if linked by Asana. Return exact page URLs used and concise findings only from official first-party pages.

raw_result.description: Work management platform with APIs for tasks, projects, users, and automation integrations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official developer docs indicate OAuth 2.0 for apps/integrations and personal access tokens for direct API access.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public developer documentation and API access are self-serve; personal access tokens and app registration are available from the developer platform without contact-sales approval evidenced in gathered docs.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API reference covers core work graph objects and actions such as tasks, projects, portfolios, goals, users, teams, webhooks, events, attachments, audit log, SCIM, and related resources.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Asana MCP server or MCP documentation was verified from the gathered official sources; absence is uncertain and should be human-reviewed.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate-limit docs were reached and indicate endpoint/domain-based limits with different standard limits for free vs paid domains, plus specialized limits such as search API and concurrent request controls. Exact numeric details were not fully captured in-chat, so this should be human-verified before publishing.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer portal is comprehensive, with dedicated guides, changelog, rate-limit documentation, and a full REST reference, which strongly signals mature public API support.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some details were inferred from browser progress rather than full extracted page text in-chat, especially the exact access-tier wording, exact rate-limit numbers, and whether an official MCP exists versus simply not being found in gathered sources.
